title: All Parties Agree on May 9 for Parliament's Inaugural Session
detail: All parties that won seats in Hungary's April 12 election agreed at consultations convened by President Tamás Sulyok to hold the new National Assembly's inaugural session on May 9 at 10:00 a.m. Péter Magyar is expected to take his oath of office as prime minister on the same day, officially concluding Hungary's institutional transfer of power. Under the Hungarian constitution, the inaugural session must occur no later than May 12. The May 9 date carries symbolic weight — it is Europe Day, the anniversary of the 1950 Schuman Declaration that launched European integration — underscoring Magyar's strongly pro-EU stance.

media.caption: All parties agree on May 9 for Hungary's new parliament inaugural session — Europe Day
